description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the debian:testing Docker image. Define two stages: build and publish. In the build stage add a build job that runs before_script commands: apt-get update, apt-get install -y gnupg devscripts, echo $MERGETB_GPG_KEY | base64 -di | gpg --import, echo "DEBSIGN_KEYID=$MERGETB_GPG_KEY_ID" >> /etc/devscripts.conf; then script commands: mk-build-deps --install --tool=\"$TOOL_ARGS\" debian/control, rm -f *build-deps*.deb, ./build-deb.sh; store the build/ directory as an artifact that never expires and set variable TOOL_ARGS to apt-get -o Debug::pkgProblemResolver=yes --no-install-recommends --yes. In the publish stage add a publish job that runs before_script: apt update, apt install curl -y, curl -L https://gitlab.com/mergetb/devops/pkg/raw/master/add-deb.sh > add-deb.sh, chmod +x add-deb.sh; it depends on build, runs only on tags, and executes script commands: find . -name \"*.deb\" | RELEASE=bullseye xargs -n 1 ./add-deb.sh and find . -name \"*.deb\" | RELEASE=buster xargs -n 1 ./add-deb.sh to upload Debian packages. Both jobs use runners tagged merge and docker.
